item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea…I wonder if that would work…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With or without bones?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i'd like to order a uhhhh pepperoni, no 682-killing juice please, thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment!

I agree, good thing it was pizza delivery. I never thought about how bad it could have potentially been if the dude had a different job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps then we would have had another IKEA-SCP-like situation! That's what we call 'een geluk bij een ongeluk' in Dutch; a piece of good luck that comes with the bad luck. In this case, AT LEAST it's just pizzas. Anyway, thanks for the compliments!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good concept and article, and I really like the implication that his mom just really wants the guy to clean up his act from beyond the grave. Kinda lucky it was pizza delivery he fell into and not a Wal-Mart or something, otherwise the Foundation might have had to be a little more aggressive, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off, thanks for your nice compliments!

I kinda want to read a tale regarding the 'off the record' discussion that the researcher and -2 have.

I've actually been working on a tale about -2 for a long time now. It's kind of been collecting digital dust though. Maybe I'll go ahead and finish it soon…you've really encouraged me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment and the upvote! :D

I kinda want to read a tale regarding the 'off the record' discussion that the researcher and -2 have. You could argue that having something like that would be way too much closure for an SCP, but dang it man, I like it when the Foundation tries to help people.

I'm of the same mind—I headcanon the Foundation as being as charitable as it can be to people who are affected by anomalies that pose little harm. It doesn't do them any bad to help these people out.

To the Foundation, they're just glad one of these pizza's isn't trying to snap people's necks or end the world.

True! I also like to think of the weird scenarios Foundation staff encounter when handling anomalies. In this case, it's dealing with strange pizzas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP a lot, because it takes what could've just been a fun -J article and turns it into a genuine interesting idea.

I think that -2 is humanized really well throughout the piece, and I kinda want to read a tale regarding the 'off the record' discussion that the researcher and -2 have. You could argue that having something like that would be way too much closure for an SCP, but dang it man, I like it when the Foundation tries to help people.

As someone else pointed out, there's also a really good contrast between the the Foundation and the general populace. To -2, this is the worst thing that could've happened to him. To the Foundation, they're just glad one of these pizza's isn't trying to snap people's necks or end the world.

So yeah, this gets my +1.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Generally, I am all for crosslinking this article, but I do agree with Zyn in that the one proposed here is a bit too far fetched; the hostile ghosts in 450 are very different in nature from the mother as implied here. Perhaps I would have liked the footnote without the link, but I understand that this would kind of invalidate the point of a WikiWalk edit. So I'll decline as well.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… I'm not too sure about this, mainly because the connection isn't solid enough for my liking. Furthermore, I don't know if it's made super clear in the article that there's a ghost at work, and the focus is meant to be more on the guy and the pizzas, not so much the mother.

I think I prefer to decline the crosslink. If my co-author disagrees, I will defer to them.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: authors vetoed edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP is about a ghost who tries to get her son on track by messing with his pizza delivery. I propose crosslinking to SCP-450, which is an SCP about ghosts.

Editing this:

Yeah, she passed away right before I started on my first day at this job.

To this:

Yeah, she passed away1 right before I started on my first day at this job.

Footnotes
1. Involvement of one or more spectral entities is suspected in the SCP-3275 phenomena.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, I'd like one buffalo chicken pizza, boneless, hold the neutrons.

Could be an interesting way to test when specifically this manifests… and with hilarious consequences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh no, chicken on pizza is the best. :D I prefer it with bacon and white sauce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: now I feel like I'm being too fancy for liking pizza with chicken and pesto on it ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dead serious, "Canadian bacon, olives, and jalapenos" is one of my family's standard pizza orders. Mushrooms or pepperoni are sometimes substituted for Canadian bacon. Either way, it is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It actually didn't even really taste that bad, but maybe my pizza standards are just low.

It's hard to screw up Pizza. I mean, you kinda have to be trying to make it taste horrible without skipping the "cook it" step.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's true that I ordered it with these toppings on purpose! That it looked bad was just a nice bonus for my picture. It actually didn't even really taste that bad, but maybe my pizza standards are just low.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope it tasted pretty good, because it looks terrible.

Fortunately, that's the point! The pizza was ordered specifically to feature in this article.

Admittedly, if I recall correctly, that was one of the reasons I was totally up for co-authoring this. Scented_Shadow was willing to purposely order a gross-looking pizza to serve as the image, and I can't help but admire that willingness to put something cool in an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image was taken by myself, with my own phone, of a pizza I ordered.

I hope it tasted pretty good, because it looks terrible.

As a former pizza cook, I'd honestly have been more than a bit ashamed to send that out for delivery. The toppings are an absolute mess, what appear to be bacon strips are practically charcoal, the crust is unevenly cooked…it makes me sad to look at it. Plus, bacon should always be small pieces, not strips, so it can be spread evenly over the whole pizza.

(Not joking even a little bit. Even when we were slammed we never made a pizza that looked like that. But we were consistently one of the highest rated shops of the local chain I worked for.)

((EDIT: Which, now that I think about it, bringing that up is funny, because I started reading the SCP wiki during that time period, and introduced a number of my coworkers to it via the shop computer during slow periods.))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the reason those conversations are off-record is probably because they're about 95% the Foundation agent just patiently explaining that no, this is not a horrible thing all things considered and yes, you're going to be okay. Just clean your room tomorrow, kid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could read the off-the-record conversations.

"Kid, don't worry. You're actually really lucky. Most of the weird stuff we deal with is far worse than wrong pizzas. I know this might seem like the end of the world to you, but, well… Just take our word for it, it isn't."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! We tried to imply something with the words chosen in the interview without stating it explicitly. Most people catch on or at least like it though, so that's good. Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the development here: First the guy whose deliveries all go wrong and everybody believing that it is his fault, then the connection to his personal hygiene and tidiness. I personally do not need the interview at the end, but I understand that understanding the reason for all these mishaps is a gain for some people. Upvoted.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Glad you liked it. I was thinking of putting an experiment with '458 in the test logs, but we decided it wasn't really necessary.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Tuice Tayna, instead of replying to your own comments, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Please Ignore that typo

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: This makes me reminds me of the other pizza SCP 458, but like more creative and the opposite effect. Upvoted

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: No worries! This is my first piece as well, and I know I would want to know more about others!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Good point. There'd definitely be some sort of surveillance and safe-guards. Thanks for the answers, just trying to get a grip on what happens in these scenarios.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I see where you're coming from. However, I think it would be more of a hassle if he had to be explained what was going on every time because they keep making him forget.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: That makes sense. I'm extremely new, so take this with half of a grain of salt, but he seems somewhat aware of the Foundation's (though it's clear he doesn't know the Foundation's nature or anything of that sort) intervention in his personal life, or at least them paying his employer. I'd think that'd be a bit of a risk. (It's still definitely a +1, just one of those little details that niggle at you.)

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Glad you liked it! And no, he wasn't. There was originally a note addressing this at the end of the interview, but it was scrapped. Basically, the reasoning is that '-2 is clearly already aware how weird it is, and if you erase his memory he will only continue to be surprised by it every time.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I liked it. I love the idea of taking everyday phenomena (IE wrong orders), and adding a twist to make it an SCP. (That's what I'm assuming you were going for, sorry if I'm wrong.) I'm curious though; was SCP-3275-2 administered with amnestics at any point?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel very bad still for not being able to review this while it was a draft! I suck! (I was fixing the tag search if that makes it better)

No worries you're fiiiinneee ;^;

Tag search is important!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much! As you can see from our other replies above, we were struggling with the interview for a while, so I'm glad that people generally seem to like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww I really love this one!

Makes me happy to see the foundation helping when they can, even if they are still their normal cold selves.

+1 from me, really enjoyed the character dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 3275-2's dialogue went through quite a few revisions during the editing phase. Glad you liked the character! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is just… so weird and totally off-the-wall. I really like the character of 3275-2 as well. +1 for sure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you thought the interview was unique! We were kind of in a pickle on what to do with it for a little bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I was nonplussed, because it seemed like it might just be one shitty employee fucking with the pizzas. And then the article's all, "NO BUT IT TOTALLY ISN'T THAT, WE SWEAR".

It wasn't until the state of the pizzas was linked to his personal hygiene that I really took notice. I find I like that link more than the dead mom, but overall, it's a neat effect, and I like that we've got a phenomenon centered on an individual, who is completely aware of it. I don't think I've seen an interview with that line of questioning before. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Glad ya liked it! ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel very bad still for not being able to review this while it was a draft! I suck! (I was fixing the tag search if that makes it better)

+1, I had no idea I wanted to read this so much until I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
potential spoilers
hide

ghost mum just wants pizza boy man to get his act together ;<

Thanks for the comment! And the +1! Much appreciated. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This dances with agility right up to the 'and this is why this is happening' without crossing the line. Nice job.

The idea that keeping his person and personal environment clean and neat prevents the anomaly from occurring is a charming aspect of the SCP.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This has been in the works for quite awhile!

Also I'm sick at home with allergies! /sniffffff

Big thanks to everyone who helped review this in chat and on the forums, especially tretter, who hashed out specifics with us!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is a co-authored piece by Scented_Shadow and Zyn!

Many thanks to our reviewers in chat! MrAnakinSpecter, Joreth, LadyKatie, Tufto, Jekeled, trots/tretter, and AidenEldritch!

Also everyone in the forum! http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2232578/excuse-me-this-is-the-wrong-pizza

The image was taken by myself, with my own phone, of a pizza I ordered. I digitally altered the colors slightly.
